mission.title: podman-container-tools: Feature request: simplify exposing the machine socket on OS X
mission.description: Feature request: simplify exposing the machine socket on OS X. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current podman-container-tools setup
steps.description:
Verify your podman-container-tools version and configuration:
```bash
podman --version
```
This feature requires a working podman-container-tools installation.

steps.title: Review podman-container-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

**Description**
I'd like to suggest adding a way to expose the remote machine socket (`/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock`) more easily. 

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature request: simplify exposing the machine socket on OS…
steps.description:
> The unix-to-unix over ssh forwarding has been added in my PR.
> 
>  I feel like gvproxy could be a better tool that services more than just podman; but as long it's stack is statically configured and it has flags for proxying things out of band of the VirtualNetwork's services, it will remain
```yaml
sshPort=$(ps -ef | grep -i gvproxy | grep -v grep | sed 's/.*ssh-port //')

ssh -i ~/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p $sshPort -L'/tmp/podman.sock:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock' -N core@localhost
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Feature request: simplify exposing the machine socket on OS…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> The unix-to-unix over ssh forwarding has been added in my PR.
> 
>  I feel like gvproxy could be a better tool that services more than just podman; but as long it's stack is statically configured and it has flags for proxying things out of band of the VirtualNetwork's services, it will remain service-locked to podman.
codeSnippets:
sshPort=$(ps -ef | grep -i gvproxy | grep -v grep | sed 's/.*ssh-port //')

ssh -i ~/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p $sshPort -L'/tmp/podman.sock:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock' -N core@localhost
